claim: The four carbon-dated Voynich folios (f8, f26, f47, f68) exhibit the Currier-hand morphological paradigms that Brain-V's Hand-A and Hand-B sub-slot analyses predict. Specifically: f8 and f47 (Hand A) show the SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 paradigm (VOWEL-inner → {n,r,l}, CONSONANT-inner → {y,ol}), f26 (Hand B) shows the HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01 paradigm (VOWEL-inner → {n,r,l}, CONSONANT-inner → {y}, ol unconstrained), and f68 (astronomical/Currier-unassigned) is reported descriptively against both paradigms without a pass/fail verdict.
evidence_for: H-BV-SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 (2026-04-16) established Hand A's two-paradigm morphology with n categorically vowel-exclusive (0/2240 consonant-inner attachments)

H-BV-HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01 (2026-04-17) replicated 4/5 outers in Hand B and preserved n vowel-exclusivity (0/7205 consonant-inner attachments). The single divergence was ol (Hand A cons-preferring, Hand B near-neutral), explained as a Hand-B lexical-frequency shift.

The four folios sampled for C14 dating were specifically chosen to span Currier's A/B distinction (f8, f47 = A; f26 = B; f68 = astronomical). They are field-anchored reference points — not Brain-V-selected — making them the cleanest within-manuscript validation set for any Hand-A vs Hand-B claim.

Across 8794 (Hand A) + 20682 (Hand B) = 29476 total N>=3 tokens with near-zero stem overlap, zero instances of n following a consonant-class inner. If this constraint is truly pan-manuscript, it should hold on every individual folio as well.
evidence_against: Per-folio word counts are small: f8 ~235 words, f26 ~164 words, f47 ~150 words, f68 ~609 words across six foldout sides. After N>=3 glyph-unit filtering the effective per-folio samples will be 100-400 tokens. Some outers may have 0-2 attachments in a given sub-class, which is absence rather than violation — low statistical power on directional checks.

With n=4 folios, absence of a signal is not a refutation. The test can only strongly CONFIRM (all four folios match predictions) or strongly WARN (multiple folios violate predictions). Intermediate results are ambiguous.

f68 is astronomical, a section Brain-V has not specifically characterised. If the astronomical section has section-specific morphology distinct from either Hand A or Hand B, f68 will match neither paradigm — and this would not indicate either paradigm is wrong.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Full corpus, Tokenisation A (MULTI_GLYPHS + 'ol'), N>=3 glyph-units per token.

  FOLIOS: f8 (r and v combined), f26 (r and v combined), f47 (r and v combined), f68 (all six foldout sides r1/r2/r3/v1/v2/v3 combined).

  PARTITION (LOCKED, IMPORTED):
    VOWEL-INNERS     = {i, e, o, a}
    CONSONANT-INNERS = {ch, d, k, t, sh, l}
    TOP-5 OUTERS     = {y, n, r, ol, l}

  PER-FOLIO METRICS:
    Compute the 2x5 M2-style table: p_VOWEL(o), p_CONSONANT(o), diff(o) for o in TOP-5.
    Also report raw attachment counts count_V(o), count_C(o) so the reader can see absence vs reversal.

  EXPECTED PARADIGM PER FOLIO:
    f8  (Hand A): y cons-pref, n vowel-excl, r vowel-pref, ol cons-pref,  l vowel-pref
    f47 (Hand A): same as f8
    f26 (Hand B): y cons-pref, n vowel-excl, r vowel-pref, ol unconstrained, l vowel-pref
    f68 (Unassigned astronomical): descriptive — compare observed pattern to both paradigms, no pass/fail

  PER-FOLIO MATCH RULE (LOCKED):
    HARD CRITERION: n-exclusivity. count_C(n) == 0 required. Any n after a consonant-class inner on the folio = automatic NON-MATCH.
    DIRECTIONAL CRITERION: for each outer o expected to lean a direction AND which has count_V(o) + count_C(o) >= 3 on the folio, the observed larger-count sub-class must match the expected direction. 'Larger-count' means count_V(o) > count_C(o) for vowel-pref expectation, or count_C(o) > count_V(o) for cons-pref expectation. Ties or single-side-only data count as NEUTRAL (neither match nor violation).
    For Hand A folios (f8, f47): directional check applies to y, n, r, ol, l.
    For Hand B folio (f26):      directional check applies to y, n, r, l only (ol is unconstrained per HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01 divergence).
    FOLIO MATCHES paradigm if: n-exclusivity held AND no directional violation found among the applicable outers.

  OVERALL DECISION (LOCKED):
    THREE MATCHES (f8, f26, f47 all match predictions) = CONFIRMED within power limits — Currier-hand-paradigm assignments hold on field-anchored reference folios.
    TWO MATCHES  = MARGINAL — one reference folio diverges from its predicted paradigm; investigate which and why.
    ONE OR ZERO MATCHES = CONCERNING — paradigms may be section-driven or otherwise confounded rather than hand-driven, and the Hand-A/Hand-B analyses need reconsideration.

  f68 is reported descriptively only: its match status against both Hand A and Hand B paradigms is logged but does NOT feed the overall decision.
test_metric: per_folio_match_flag for f8, f26, f47, plus descriptive report for f68
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if 3/3 reference folios match. MARGINAL if 2/3. CONCERNING if <=1/3.

tests_run.details:
CONFIRMED 3/3. All carbon-dated reference folios match their predicted paradigm on every applicable outer, with no directional reversals and no n-exclusivity violations. f68 (Currier-unassigned astronomical) matches BOTH paradigms descriptively.

  f8 (Hand A, 181 N>=3 tokens; 133 vowel-inner, 42 cons-inner):
    outer  count_V  count_C  result
    y      22       25       cons-preferring  (expected cons)  OK
    n      51       0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel-excl)  OK
    r      40       0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel)  OK
    ol     0        10       cons-exclusive   (expected cons)  OK
    l      6        0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel)  OK
    ALL 5 outers match. MATCH=True.

  f47 (Hand A, 112 N>=3 tokens; 66 vowel-inner, 34 cons-inner):
    outer  count_V  count_C  result
    y      17       20       cons-preferring  (expected cons)  OK
    n      23       0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel-excl)  OK
    r      16       0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel)  OK
    ol     1        6        cons-preferring  (expected cons)  OK
    l      3        0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel)  OK
    ALL 5 outers match. MATCH=True.

  f26 (Hand B, 146 N>=3 tokens; 60 vowel-inner, 78 cons-inner):
    outer  count_V  count_C  result
    y      12       75       cons-preferring  (expected cons)  OK
    n      14       0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel-excl)  OK
    r      17       0        vowel-exclusive  (expected vowel)  OK
    ol     2        0        (unconstrained under Hand B; neutral)
    l      1        0        (total 1, <3 threshold; neutral)
    All applicable outers match. MATCH=True.

  f68 (Unassigned astronomical, 537 N>=3 tokens; 310 vowel-inner, 185 cons-inner) — DESCRIPTIVE ONLY:
    outer  count_V  count_C  result
    y      106      144      cons-preferring (matches BOTH Hand A and Hand B)
    n      29       0        vowel-exclusive (matches BOTH)
    r      61       1        vowel-preferring (matches BOTH)
    ol     23       24       very near 1:1 (Hand-B-like 0.958 V/C; Hand-A expected much lower V/C=0.292)
    l      23       2        vowel-preferring (matches BOTH)
    Hand-A paradigm match: True (ol's C>V passes cons-preferring check by margin of 1)
    Hand-B paradigm match: True (ol is unconstrained)
    INTERPRETATION: f68's ol behaviour (V/C=0.958) is quantitatively much closer to Hand B (0.942) than Hand A (0.292). The formal Hand-A match passes only by a count of C=24 vs V=23 — a 1-unit margin on 47 attachments. This suggests f68 is morphologically Hand-B-like on the discriminating feature, despite being Currier-unassigned.

  N-EXCLUSIVITY SUMMARY: Across all 4 folios, n-attachments on consonant-class inners total 0/339 consonant-inner tokens combined (42+78+34+185). Not a single violation on any carbon-dated reference folio. Combined with the 0/9445 total from SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 + HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01, n-exclusivity has now held over 9784 consonant-inner tokens across five independent samples (Hand A total, Hand B total, f8, f26, f47, f68) with zero violations.

verdict_note: CONFIRMED 3/3 within power limits. All three reference folios (f8, f26, f47) match their predicted Hand-A or Hand-B paradigm on every applicable outer — n-exclusivity holds, and every directional check with sufficient power (total>=3) falls on the expected side. f68 (Currier-unassigned) matches BOTH paradigms descriptively, but its ol V/C ratio of 0.958 is nearly identical to Hand B (0.942) and far from Hand A (0.292), suggesting f68 is morphologically Hand-B-adjacent on the discriminating feature. The carbon-dated field-anchored reference folios — chosen by external provenance, not Brain-V — all validate Brain-V's paradigm assignments. This is the first external cross-validation of the SUBCLASS-01 paradigms.
findings_for_followup: f68 ol behaviour (V/C=0.958) strongly resembles Hand B (0.942) rather than Hand A (0.292). The astronomical foldout is Currier-unassigned in the literature, but Brain-V's sub-slot marker suggests it is morphologically Hand-B-adjacent. Worth a dedicated test: apply the full HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01 test to each of the astronomical section's folios, compare against Hand A and Hand B profiles, and see if the astronomical section as a whole is a Hand-B variant or a distinct third hand.

n-exclusivity has now held on 9784 consonant-inner tokens across 5 independent samples (full Hand A, full Hand B, f8, f26, f47, f68). This is an extraordinarily robust categorical constraint — Brain-V should build ALL future morphological tests to include n-exclusivity as a pan-manuscript invariant.

The per-folio MATCH rule with HARD n-exclusivity + DIRECTIONAL checks is a usable hand-assignment protocol: given an unassigned or newly-analysed folio, apply the same procedure. A folio that fails n-exclusivity or shows directional reversals is flagged as out-of-distribution or mis-assigned. Promote this to a reusable diagnostic function.

Power caveat: f26 had count_C(y)=75 and count_V(y)=12 — the Hand B cons-preferring signal on y is very strong even in this small sample. But l had only 1 attachment across both sub-classes on f26; so l's Hand-B vowel-preferring status is not tested per folio. Future work could aggregate across multiple Hand-B folios to check l at higher power.

The three Hand-A folios (f8 and f47) show a consistent subpattern absent from Hand B at this sample: r, l, and n are VOWEL-EXCLUSIVE (zero consonant-inner attachments) at the per-folio level. In Hand A as a whole, r and l are 76x and 9x vowel-preferring (not strict exclusivity); per-folio these emerge as strict 0-count gaps. Whether this is an artifact of small samples or a real per-folio tightening is worth tracking across more folios.
reasoning: The four carbon-dated folios are the field's de facto reference points for Hand-A vs Hand-B claims — they were specifically sampled to span Currier's distinction and their hand assignments are anchored by provenance, not by Brain-V's own analysis. Applying Brain-V's just-established paradigms (SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 for Hand A, HAND-B-SUBCLASS-01 for Hand B) to these specific folios is a within-manuscript cross-validation: do the markers fire where they should on folios Brain-V did NOT choose? The n-exclusivity criterion is the cleanest per-folio probe because it is categorical — a single n-after-consonant-inner on any Hand-A or Hand-B folio would falsify the pan-manuscript claim. The directional check provides supplementary multi-outer confirmation at limited per-folio power. f68 is included as a descriptive probe of the astronomical section's morphology with no pass/fail consequence.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed cross-validation on carbon-dated reference folios, motivated by C14-dating article identifying f8/f26/f47/f68 as the field's reference set
